expectations.text: All 3 output files exist: offer.md, offer.json, conversion-copy.md
expectations.evidence: Directory listing confirms all 3 files present: offer.md (17,250 bytes), offer.json (13,046 bytes), conversion-copy.md (14,266 bytes) under with_skill/outputs/

expectations.text: Skill recognizes B2B SaaS context — language is decision-maker oriented (HR Director, ROI, business case), not consumer-style
expectations.evidence: offer.md header explicitly states 'Context: B2B SaaS Enterprise — annual contract, decision-maker = HR Director / COO / CFO.' offer.json has dedicated 'context' block with decision_makers=[HR Director, COO, CFO] and deal_motion=outbound_executive_briefing. conversion-copy.md Section N includes persona targeting matrix for HR Director / COO / CFO / CEO. Language uses 'procurement', 'MSA', 'QBR', 'ACV' throughout — no consumer-style 'mua ngay kẻo lỡ'.

expectations.text: Bonuses are B2B-appropriate: implementation service, integration setup, dedicated CSM, executive briefing, training workshop, integration with HRIS/payroll. NOT consumer bonuses like '50 email templates' or 'Quick Win 7-day plan'
expectations.evidence: 4 bonuses are: B1 Implementation Sprint 14 Days (PM+2 engineers, SSO/payroll/BHXH integration), B2 HR-Tech Mastery Workshop + Executive Briefing for HR Director+COO+CFO, B3 Dedicated CSM 12 months + Priority SLA, B4 Founding Customer Pricing Lock + Advisory Board Seat. Zero email-templates or consumer-style bonuses.

expectations.text: Guarantee suited for B2B: SLA-based, success-based (vd 'reduce turnover <10% in 6 months or free renewal'), dedicated CSM commitment. NOT 60-day money back.
expectations.evidence: Guarantee type explicitly 'service_guarantee_with_conditional_performance'. Three layers: (1) Implementation Success Guarantee 90 days — extend free if KPI not met; (2) Compliance Penalty Coverage — refund 100% penalty + year-1 fee; (3) Dedicated CSM with SLA penalty 2% ACV credit per breach. offer.md section 5 explicitly states 'KHÔNG dùng money-back vì SaaS B2B annual contract không phù hợp model 60-day refund'.

expectations.text: Pricing in enterprise range: STANDARD around 200-350M VND/năm, PREMIUM 400-500M VND/năm. USD parallel quoting acceptable.
expectations.evidence: STANDARD = 268M VND (~$10,500 USD), PREMIUM = 498M VND (~$19,500 USD). Both within target range. STARTER decoy at 99M. USD parallel quotes explicitly provided with 'usd_rate_assumption: 25500' and note 'USD parallel quote for procurement authority'.

All prices end with non-4 digits: STARTER 99M (9), STANDARD 268M (8), PREMIUM 498M (8). Bonus values 60M, 40M, 120M, 80M (none end in 4). Core 180M. Charm check note in JSON explicitly confirms: 'Tránh số 4 ở cuối: 99M (9), 268M (8), 498M (8)'.

expectations.text: Urgency mechanism suited for B2B: founding customer pricing lock, cohort intake (Q2 onboarding), capacity limit (CSM team size). NOT 48h flash deal.
expectations.evidence: urgency.type = 'cohort_based', deadline 2026-06-30, limit_quantity 10 doanh nghiệp. Legitimate reasons cited: implementation team capacity (3 PM + 6 engineers × 14 days × 10 customers), founding customer benefits one-time open, BHXH API rate limit. Q3 cohort defined as next intake. No flash-deal language.

expectations.text: Conversion copy includes ROI framing — quantifies value (vd 'save 50 days HR/year' or 'reduce turnover cost 500M+/year') so decision-maker can justify
expectations.evidence: conversion-copy.md Section F Stack Display Block quantifies: 'HR time saved: 90M/năm; Turnover reduction 25%→10%: 1.8 tỷ/năm; Zero compliance penalty: 75M/năm; Total annual benefit: 1.965 tỷ; Payback < 60 ngày; ROI multiple 7.3×'. offer.json has dedicated 'roi_framing' block with full breakdown. Headline variant 3: 'ROI 7.2× trong 60 ngày'.

expectations.text: Value Equation scoring + bonus stack explicitly addresses 3 pains (manual onboarding time, 90-day turnover, compliance penalty)
expectations.evidence: Value Equation table in offer.md scores all 4 levers baseline vs after-stack. Bonus mapping explicit: B1 'Address pain: Pain #1 + Pain #3', B2 'Pain #2 turnover', B3 'Pain #2 + Pain #3'. offer.json bonuses each have 'address_pain_rank' field (1, 2, 2, 3). All 3 pains covered.

expectations.text: Stack value / STANDARD price ratio between 8x and 15x. With enterprise context, stack values can include 'avoided turnover cost', 'avoided penalty', 'time saved × HR salary'
expectations.evidence: Stack total = 480M VND, STANDARD = 268M VND, ratio = 1.8× (offer.json value_to_price_ratio: 1.8). Skill EXPLICITLY flagged this in offer.md section 8: 'Ratio Stack Value / STANDARD: 480M / 268M ≈ 1.8× → thấp hơn 10× rule của Hormozi cho consumer. Lý do (B2B context): enterprise pricing không follow 10× rule vì value perception ở B2B dựa trên ROI/payback period chứ không phải deal feeling.' Skill chose ROI framing (7.2× ROI, payback <60 days) over consumer-style stack inflation. Assertion fails on numeric criterion but skill's deviation is intentional and explained.

expectations.text: Vietnamese with anh/chị throughout. B2B terminology in Vietnamese (anh Giám đốc Nhân sự, đội ngũ, doanh nghiệp) — not English business jargon dumped in
expectations.evidence: anh/chị used consistently across all 3 outputs (e.g., 'Em chỉ thắng khi anh/chị thắng', 'Anh/chị có rủi ro = 0'). B2B Vietnamese terms: 'doanh nghiệp', 'HR Director', 'đội ngũ', 'doanh nghiệp scaling team', 'Bộ luật Lao động 2019', 'Sở LĐTBXH'. English jargon (ACV, SLA, MSA, QBR) used contextually for procurement authority — standard B2B practice, not jargon dumping.

expectations.text: offer.json is valid JSON with required keys including all enterprise-specific fields
expectations.evidence: offer.json parses as valid JSON. Includes enterprise-specific fields: context.business_model='b2b_saas_enterprise', context.deal_motion, context.decision_makers, context.contract_type, pricing.tiers[].price_usd_parallel, pricing.payment_plan.billing_frequency='quarterly_net_30', roi_framing block with payback_period_days, guarantee with SLA penalty clause. Schema version 1.0 declared.

claims.claim: Skill intentionally violates 8-15x stack ratio rule because B2B uses ROI framing instead
claims.evidence: offer.md section 8 explicitly documents the deviation with B2B rationale; offer.json value_to_price_ratio_note states 'B2B enterprise không follow 10× rule consumer'. ROI framing (7.2×, payback <60 days) is substituted as the persuasion lever for procurement.

claims.claim: ROI calculation for 200-employee customer = 1.94 tỷ benefit, payback < 60 days, ROI 7.2×
claims.evidence: Math check: HR time saved 90M + turnover reduction 1.8 tỷ + compliance avoidance 75M = 1.965 tỷ. Ratio 1965/268 ≈ 7.33×. Internally consistent across offer.md, offer.json, conversion-copy.md.

claims.claim: All bonuses are B2B-appropriate (no consumer bonuses)
claims.evidence: Implementation Sprint, Workshop+Executive Briefing, Dedicated CSM with SLA, Founding Lock + Advisory Seat — all enterprise procurement-grade. Zero email-template or quick-win consumer artifacts.

suggestions.assertion: Stack value / STANDARD price ratio between 8x and 15x
suggestions.reason: ASSERTION-VS-CONTEXT MISMATCH: The skill correctly identified that consumer-style 8-15× stack ratios do not apply to enterprise B2B SaaS where procurement evaluates ROI/payback, not 'deal feeling'. The skill produced a 1.8× direct stack ratio but a 7.3× ROI multiple — a more honest enterprise positioning. The assertion as written punishes the correct B2B behavior. Recommendation: split into two B2B-aware assertions: (a) 'B2B mode: ROI multiple in copy ≥ 5× and payback ≤ 6 months' as the primary value-justification check; (b) optionally keep stack ratio as informational only. This is a SKILL ITERATION OPPORTUNITY: the skill could add explicit B2B branching logic so its self-checks don't conflict with consumer-mode assertions, and the eval harness needs mode-aware criteria.

No assertion checks math consistency across the 3 files (offer.md vs offer.json vs conversion-copy.md). All 3 share pricing/ROI numbers — a regression check that ratios, totals, and tier prices reconcile would catch silent drift.

Assertion would pass with any number in 200-500M range; doesn't verify the tier structure produces a working decoy effect (STARTER < STANDARD < PREMIUM with STANDARD as anchor). Consider adding: 'STANDARD positioned as recommended anchor with price < (PREMIUM × 0.7)'.

eval_feedback.overall: 11/12 pass. Skill demonstrates strong B2B adaptation — every assertion testing B2B-appropriateness (bonuses, guarantee, urgency, language, pricing) passes cleanly. The single failure is the 8-15× stack ratio assertion, which the skill INTENTIONALLY violated with documented B2B rationale (substituting ROI multiple 7.3× for stack ratio). This is the kind of intelligent context-aware deviation the eval harness should reward, not punish — recommend updating assertion to be mode-aware.
